message:
feat(server): archive git maintenance worker + am doctor pack-archive CLI (br-1y8ym)

Background repack worker with configurable interval and disable flag,
plus one-shot `am doctor pack-archive` subcommand for manual invocation.
Includes acceptance tests for disable-flag and interval-floor enforcement.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"br-1y8ym","title":"Archive git maintenance: background repack + am doctor pack-archive CLI","description":"## Why

After the 2026-04-19 archive-health incident, PR #A added `gc.auto=256` to the
archive's git config so auto-repack fires during normal commit paths. That
stops fresh loose-object accumulation, but it does not proactively clean up
the existing backlog on long-running servers — and some deployments may not
hit the auto-gc threshold often.

## What

Two companion pieces:

1. `mcp_agent_mail_server::maintenance` module
   - Spawns a background task that runs once at startup (after a short delay
     so it doesn't interfere with cold-start probes) and then every 30
     minutes.
   - Invokes `git maintenance run --task=loose-objects --task=incremental-repack`
     on the archive's `.git`, with a low `ionice`/`nice` so it doesn't
     compete with live request handling.
   - Logs kept/removed/size-reclaimed numbers at info.
   - Respects `AM_ARCHIVE_MAINTENANCE_INTERVAL_SECS` (default 1800) and
     `AM_ARCHIVE_MAINTENANCE_DISABLED=1` as env overrides.

2. `am doctor pack-archive` CLI subcommand
   - One-shot manual invocation that runs the same maintenance cycle.
   - Useful after big bulk imports or incident recovery.
   - Prints a table: before/after loose count, pack count, disk use.

## Acceptance

- New unit tests for the maintenance scheduler (interval boundary, disable
  flag, error handling).
- Manual test: on a staging archive with 10k+ loose objects, observe
  auto-cleanup within one maintenance cycle.
- No regression in existing archive commit throughput (benchmark the write
  path during active maintenance — expect <5% overhead).

## Touchpoints

- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/lib.rs` — register new mod + background
  thread registration near `ack_ttl::start` / `cleanup::start`.
- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/maintenance.rs` — new module.
- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs` — add `doctor pack-archive`
  subcommand, wire to the same maintenance function (sync variant).
- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/src/config.rs` — two new env var readers.

## Out of scope

- Rewriting the archive format (that's bead F).
- Any change to how commits are created.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-19T23:58:18.506761150Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:16:06.260883308Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["archive","gc","performance"]}

   - One-shot manual invocation that runs the same maintenance cycle.

   - Prints a table: before/after loose count, pack count, disk use.

## Acceptance

  flag, error handling).

  auto-cleanup within one maintenance cycle.

  path during active maintenance — expect <5% overhead).

## Touchpoints

- `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/lib.rs` — register new mod + background

- Rewriting the archive format (that's bead F).
- Any change to how commits are created.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-19T23:58:18.506761150Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:26:24.159947797Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T04:26:24.159477967Z","close_reason":"Feature fully implemented: background maintenance worker (start/shutdown/interval), am doctor pack-archive CLI subcommand with table/JSON output, config env vars wired. Added missing acceptance tests for disable flag + interval boundary.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["archive","gc","performance"]}
 {"id":"br-1yclw","title":"T6.1: File reservation error messages parity (5 errors)","description":"5 file reservation errors: EMPTY_PATHS (paths list cannot be empty), FILE_RESERVATION_CONFLICT (on message write), NOT_FOUND (force-release), RESERVATION_ACTIVE (force-release refused), concurrent release (unable to evaluate). All messages, error codes, and data payloads must match exactly.","notes":"3 of 5 error scenarios verified matching: EMPTY_PATHS (identical), NOT_FOUND (fixed extra project key in data), RESERVATION_ACTIVE (identical). FILE_RESERVATION_CONFLICT is a feature gap (enforcement not implemented in Rust send_message — tracked separately). 'concurrent release' error does not exist in Python codebase.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-15T02:04:32.698471840Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T04:35:11.167498419Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T04:35:11.167433908Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["doc-parity"]}
 {"id":"br-1z66","title":"T2.1: Audit core::flake_triage module vs bash script — identify gaps and reusable logic","description":"## Objective
Establish a precise parity map between `core::flake_triage` capabilities and legacy `scripts/flake_triage.sh` behavior.

## Work
- Inventory existing Rust-side logic and identify script-only behavior gaps.
- Classify each gap as: implement now, intentional delta, or no-longer-needed behavior.
- Record migration decisions with rationale so implementation tasks can execute without ambiguity.

## Deliverable
A concrete parity/gap audit that de-risks implementation and prevents accidental behavior loss.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Defines explicit inputs, outputs, invariants, error taxonomy, and exit-code behavior for downstream implementation.
- Captures parity mapping to legacy behavior, including intentional deviations and technical rationale.
- Includes a concrete validation strategy (unit + integration + e2e) and detailed logging/artifact expectations for downstream tasks.
- Is self-contained and executable without referring back to external markdown plan documents.
- Leaves no ambiguous requirements that would permit incompatible implementations.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:24:29.590143573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T04:43:16.727194628Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T04:43:16.727166936Z","close_reason":"Audit complete by RubyPrairie. Gap analysis posted as comment. 3 gaps identified: artifact scanning, failure reproduction, subprocess multi-seed. All downstream beads (br-36xx, br-154k, br-1kk7) map cleanly.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":263,"issue_id":"br-1z66","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# T2.1: Audit core::flake_triage Module vs Bash Script

## What to do
Read and compare two implementations of flake triage:
1. crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/src/flake_triage.rs (existing Rust module)
2. scripts/flake_triage.sh (bash script being replaced)

## Goals of the audit
1. **Feature gap analysis**: What does the bash script do that the Rust module doesn't?
   Common gaps: artifact scanning, multi-seed detection, CLI integration.
2. **Reusable logic identification**: What functions/types in flake_triage.rs can be
   directly reused by the new CLI subcommand?
3. **Data model gaps**: Does the Rust module define the right structs for:
   - Failure artifacts (test name, seed, error message, timestamp)
   - Flake detection results (pass count, fail count, is_flaky flag)
   - Scan results (list of artifacts found, grouped by test)
4. **Interface recommendations**: What public API does flake_triage.rs need to export
   for the CLI to consume? Document needed additions.

## Deliverable
A comment on this bead summarizing:
- Reusable: [list of functions/types to reuse]
- Missing: [list of capabilities to add]
- Suggested public API for CLI consumption

## Location
crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/src/flake_triage.rs (read)
scripts/flake_triage.sh (read)
","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:29:27Z"},{"id":264,"issue_id":"br-1z66","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# T2.1: Audit core::flake_triage Module vs Bash Script

## What to do

3. **Data model gaps**: Does the Rust module define the right structs for:

   - Flake detection results (pass count, fail count, is_flaky flag)
   - Scan results (list of artifacts found, grouped by test)
4. **Interface recommendations**: What public API does flake_triage.rs need to export
   for the CLI to consume? Document needed additions.

## Deliverable
A comment on this bead summarizing:
- Reusable: [list of functions/types to reuse]

## Location
crates/mcp-agent-mail-core/src/flake_triage.rs (read)
scripts/flake_triage.sh (read)
","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:29:38Z"},{"id":265,"issue_id":"br-1z66","author":"RubyPrairie","text":"# Audit: core::flake_triage.rs vs scripts/flake_triage.sh

## Auditor: RubyPrairie (claude-code/opus-4.6)

---

## Rust Module (flake_triage.rs — 967 lines, 25+ tests)

### Capabilities:
1. **FailureContext** struct: full capture (test_name, harness_seed, e2e_seed, failure_message, failure_ts, repro_command, repro_context, env_snapshot with redaction, rss_kb, uptime_secs, category, notes). Serialize/deserialize + write_artifact().
2. **FlakeReport** struct: multi-run outcomes (RunOutcome), 4 verdicts (Stable/DeterministicFailure/Flaky/Inconclusive), failure_histogram, failing_seeds, remediation text, write_artifact().
3. **run_with_seeds()**: closure-based multi-seed runner. DEFAULT_FLAKE_SEEDS (17 values).
4. **classify_failure()**: pattern-matching for Timing/Contention/CiEnvironment/Assertion/Unknown. CI env awareness.
5. **capture_env_snapshot()**: prefix-filtered capture with secret redaction.
6. **System info**: read_rss_kb() (Linux /proc), read_uptime_secs() (OnceLock Instant).

## Bash Script (flake_triage.sh — 193 lines)

### Capabilities:
1. **Reproduce from artifact** (default mode): reads failure_context.json via python3, displays summary, interactive y/N confirmation, executes repro_command via eval.
2. **Scan for failures** (--scan [dir]): walks directory tree with find -name failure_context.json, displays numbered list with timestamp/name/category/path.
3. **Multi-seed run** (--multi-seed test N): same 17-seed corpus, extends with RANDOM if N>17, runs cargo test subprocess per seed, colored PASS/FAIL per seed, verdict (STABLE/ALWAYS FAILS/FLAKY with rate).

---

## Gap Analysis

### MISSING from Rust (bash has it):

1. **Artifact scanning** — walk directory tree, find failure_context.json files, display summary list with timestamps. (Needed for CLI --scan / downstream br-36xx)
2. **Failure reproduction from artifact** — read JSON artifact, display context, exec repro command. (Needed for CLI reproduce / downstream br-1kk7)
3. **Subprocess-based multi-seed** — spawn cargo test as subprocess per seed. Rust run_with_seeds() uses closures (in-process only). CLI needs subprocess mode for full binary startup + isolation. (Needed for downstream br-154k)
4. **Random seed extension** — bash extends seeds with RANDOM when N > 17. Rust has fixed DEFAULT_FLAKE_SEEDS only.
5. **FailureContext::from_file()** — reading/deserializing existing artifacts (Rust can write but not read back).

### INTENTIONAL DELTAS (Rust is better, keep as-is):

- No python3 dependency (native serde_json)
- In-process closure-based multi-seed for unit tests — keep, plus add subprocess for CLI
- Auto-classification is Rust-side (bash reads category from artifact)
- Environment capture + system metrics are Rust enrichment — bash lacks these

### REUSABLE (no changes needed):

- FailureContext struct + capture() + with_repro() + add_note() + write_artifact()
- FlakeReport struct + from_runs() (verdict, histogram, remediation)
- run_with_seeds() function + DEFAULT_FLAKE_SEEDS
- classify_failure() function (all 6 categories)
- capture_env_snapshot() with secret redaction
- read_rss_kb(), read_uptime_secs()
- RunOutcome, FlakeVerdict, FailureCategory enums

### ADDITIONS NEEDED (suggested public API for CLI):

```rust
/// Read a failure context from a JSON artifact file.
pub fn read_artifact(path: &Path) -> Result<FailureContext, std::io::Error>;

/// Scan a directory tree for failure_context.json artifacts.
pub fn scan_artifacts(dir: &Path) -> Vec<ScannedArtifact>;

/// A scanned failure artifact with file location and summary.
pub struct ScannedArtifact {
    pub path: PathBuf,
    pub context: FailureContext,
}

/// Run a test as a subprocess with multiple seeds.
/// Unlike run_with_seeds() (closures), this spawns cargo test.
pub fn run_multi_seed_subprocess(
    test_name: &str,
    seeds: &[u64],
    extra_packages: &[String],
) -> FlakeReport;

/// Extend seed corpus with random values (for N > DEFAULT_FLAKE_SEEDS.len()).
pub fn extend_seeds(base: &[u64], target_count: usize) -> Vec<u64>;
```

## Summary

The Rust module is a solid foundation with richer data models and classification than the bash script. The main gaps are:
- **Read-side operations** (scan, reproduce) — bash is a CLI tool, Rust is a library
- **Subprocess execution** — needed for CLI integration
- **Seed extension** — minor ergonomic gap

All 3 downstream beads (br-36xx, br-154k, br-1kk7) map cleanly to the identified gaps.
","created_at":"2026-02-12T04:43:10Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-1zvl","title":"T3.8: Add deprecation notice header in scripts/bench_cli.sh pointing to am bench","description":"## Objective
Finalize Track 3 migration by deprecating script-first benchmark invocation in `scripts/bench_cli.sh`.

## Work
- Add native-first messaging with explicit `am bench` replacements.
- Preserve compatibility fallback guidance where policy requires it.
- Keep deprecation language consistent with docs and release workflows.

## Deliverable
A clear script shim that directs operators to native benchmark commands.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Output is self-contained: clear before/after command mapping, scope boundaries, rationale, and migration decision record.
- Native `am` command path is authoritative and legacy script paths are explicitly labeled as deprecated or compatibility-only, with rollback conditions documented.
- Operator-facing troubleshooting guidance includes where to find logs/artifacts and how to reproduce failures quickly.
- CI/release/runbook surfaces that users execute are updated with no contradictory script-first guidance remaining.
- Includes a verification checklist so future maintainers can re-audit correctness without external plan documents.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WindyLynx","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:24:49.237364029Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:21:01.095258042Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T04:21:01.095238776Z","close_reason":"Completed: bench_cli shim deprecated with native am bench mapping and docs cleanup","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-1zvl","depends_on_id":"br-2x5p4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-11T23:56:06Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":266,"issue_id":"br-1zvl","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# T3.8: Add Deprecation Notice in scripts/bench_cli.sh

## What to do
Add deprecation banner and runtime warning to scripts/bench_cli.sh.

## Changes
1. Banner after shebang:
```bash
# DEPRECATED: Use `am bench` instead.
# Native Rust implementation — no hyperfine or python3 required.
# 100x faster DB seeding, built-in statistics and baseline comparison.
# Usage: am bench [--quick] [--json] [--baseline <path>]
```

2. Runtime warning:
```bash
echo "WARNING: scripts/bench_cli.sh is deprecated. Use 'am bench' instead." >&2
```

## DO NOT delete the script. Keep working for backward compatibility.

## Location
scripts/bench_cli.sh (top of file)
","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:31:07Z"},{"id":267,"issue_id":"br-1zvl","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed deprecation + native-first mapping for bench_cli. Updated scripts/bench_cli.sh with explicit deprecation header, command mapping to am bench, and runtime warning. Updated benches/BUDGETS.md and docs/SPEC-script-migration-matrix.md to remove stale script-first/am bench cli guidance and reflect migrated status. Verification: bash -n scripts/bench_cli.sh passed; grep checks show no remaining stale am bench cli references in touched files.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:20:56Z"}]}
